tasks.title: Baseline Measurement
acceptance_criteria: the runtime can report how many tools are exposed in a normal chat execution
the runtime can estimate the serialized size of the tool block before provider calls
the runtime can identify oversized tool outputs in local observability
notes: Added deterministic tool payload measurement in the agent loop
Recorded tool_count and tool_payload_bytes in local observability before provider calls
Marked oversized tool outputs in observability summaries using a fixed local threshold

tasks.title: Canonical Tool Profiles
acceptance_criteria: canonical profiles exist in a domain/runtime layer instead of the Telegram boundary
profiles cover the common execution modes such as plain chat, local files, local execution, web research, scheduling, team operations, and MCP access
profiles are explicit and testable
notes: Added canonical runtime tool profiles in internal/agent
Kept the profile list explicit and small instead of introducing a planner or classifier service
Documented that tool selection policy belongs to the runtime layer instead of Telegram

tasks.title: Main Chat Tool Selection
acceptance_criteria: the main chat execution chooses a minimal allowed-tool set before entering the loop
normal chat can run with a small profile instead of full registry exposure
filesystem, command execution, web, scheduling, and team intents can still access their required tools
notes: Main chat execution now resolves a minimal allowed-tool set from the user query before entering the loop
Plain chat can run with zero tools while filesystem, command, web, scheduler, team, and skill intents keep targeted access
Adjusted ToolRegistry filtering so nil means all tools and an empty slice means no tools

tasks.title: Cron And Team Alignment
acceptance_criteria: cron executions no longer default to an unnecessarily large tool surface
team tasks preserve explicit allowlists without falling back to broad exposure unless intentionally configured
execution behavior remains consistent across chat, cron, and team runtimes
notes: Cron execution now resolves allowed tools from the job prompt instead of broad defaults
Worker fallback tool selection no longer expands to the entire registry when a task has no explicit allowlist
spawn_agent now reuses the canonical worker tool-selection helper instead of keeping a duplicate heuristic

tasks.title: Safe Schema Pruning
acceptance_criteria: tool serialization keeps only contract-relevant schema fields such as type, properties, and required
redundant metadata like title, examples, and defaults can be removed safely
pruning is applied centrally and covered by tests
notes: Added central tool compaction in the agent loop before provider calls
Pruned tool schemas to contract-relevant fields such as type, properties, required, items, enum, and additionalProperties
Kept the pruning provider-agnostic so every LLM backend receives the same compact tool payload

tasks.title: Short Tool Descriptions
acceptance_criteria: MCP tool descriptions are reduced to short functional summaries
local tool descriptions stay concise and contract-oriented
no runtime path reintroduces long repeated provider or server boilerplate into descriptions
notes: Shortened tool descriptions centrally before prompt exposure
Reduced MCP-discovered descriptions to a compact functional sentence without repeated server boilerplate
Covered the compaction behavior with agent and MCP tests

tasks.title: MCP Surface Reduction
acceptance_criteria: MCP tools are grouped behind explicit profile or allowlist decisions
a normal chat execution does not automatically receive the full discovered MCP surface
configured MCP allowlists remain respected
notes: Default intent-based tool selection does not expose MCP tools unless they are explicitly allowed
Added tests proving that ordinary chat stays free of mcp_* tools while explicit MCP tools are still preserved
MCP remains available through explicit allowlists instead of contaminating every execution surface

tasks.title: Tool Output Compaction
acceptance_criteria: the runtime detects oversized tool results before appending them to history
raw output can remain available locally while a compact summary is injected into the conversation
the history stays deterministic and debuggable
notes: Oversized tool outputs are now compacted before being appended to active loop history
Raw oversized outputs are preserved in a local SQLite observability artifact table for traceability
Compacted history entries include deterministic metadata such as total size and optional artifact_id

tasks.title: Type-Specific Output Policies
acceptance_criteria: command output can be truncated or summarized deterministically
file reads can preserve the useful head/body shape without dumping the full file into history by default
MCP outputs follow the same compacting rules instead of bypassing them
notes: Applied deterministic output policies for heavy tool results using bounded head and tail previews
The same compaction path now covers run_command, read_file, and MCP outputs because it runs in the shared loop
Bounded compacted output prevents repeated large tool results from exploding prompt growth across iterations

tasks.title: Lightweight Observability
acceptance_criteria: local observability records the tool count for an execution
local observability records the approximate serialized size of the exposed tool block
local observability records raw-versus-compacted output size for oversized tool results
notes: Local observability already records tool_count and tool_payload_bytes for each loop run
Oversized tool outputs now record raw_chars, compacted_chars, oversized=true, and artifact_id when available
The full oversized payload is preserved in operational_artifacts while /ops can still surface the compact operational summary

tasks.title: Provider-Friendly Prompt Stability
acceptance_criteria: tool ordering and serialization are stable for the same profile
the implementation does not depend on provider-specific caching APIs to be correct
documentation states that prompt stability is intentional but secondary to correctness
notes: Compacted tool payloads are now sorted by tool name before provider calls
Equivalent executions produce a more stable serialized tool block for provider-side prompt caching
The implementation remains provider-agnostic and does not depend on provider-specific cache APIs

tasks.title: Documentation Finalization
docs: task references used in the completed tasks
acceptance_criteria: canonical docs explain that tools are exposed by minimum necessary profile
docs explain that oversized tool output should not pollute active history
recurring implementation traps are captured in docs/LEARNINGS.md when relevant
notes: Canonical docs now explain minimal tool exposure, stable compact tool payloads, and local artifact preservation for oversized tool output
The roadmap state for tool token reduction is fully updated through T11
Validated the final integrated state with go test ./...
